title: Find funding programs for jobs, skill-building, and community-building projects - Canada.ca
content:
## Find government funding programs and grants for: Jobs, skills training and social development projects
Explore funding opportunities  (also known as grants and contributions ) to help you fund projects that support jobs, training, or social development in Canada.
Register to get emails about funding opportunities

## Funding opportunities
- Accessible Canada Accessible Canada – National AccessAbility Week Funding – 2027 to 2029 Funding opportunity that support projects that promote the implementation of the Accessible Canada Act and its regulations through National Accessibility Week (NAAW) activities delivered in 2027, 2028 and in 2029. up to $180,000 or  up to $360,000 Accepting applications until July 28, 2026, at 3:00 p.m. Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) Social Development and Community Building
Accessible Canada – National AccessAbility Week Funding – 2027 to 2029
Funding opportunity that support projects that promote the implementation of the Accessible Canada Act and its regulations through National Accessibility Week (NAAW) activities delivered in 2027, 2028 and in 2029.
up to $180,000 or  up to $360,000
- Accepting applications until July 28, 2026, at 3:00 p.m. Eastern Daylight Time (EDT)
- Social Development and Community Building
- Compensation for Employers of Reservists Program Compensation for Employers of Reservists Financial support to offset operational costs incured when reservist employees deploy on full-time operational activity, or participate in military skills training for a minimum of 30 days. Funding amounts are determined based on the assessment of the application. Accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Compensation for Employers of Reservists Program
Compensation for Employers of Reservists
Financial support to offset operational costs incured when reservist employees deploy on full-time operational activity, or participate in military skills training for a minimum of 30 days.
Funding amounts are determined based on the assessment of the application.
- Accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy (CAS) Investments in Training Equipment Funding for projects that demonstrate a need for trades training equipment and materials that meet industry standards or investments in new technology to train pre-apprentices and apprentices in Red Seal trades. Up to $5,000,000 per project Accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy (CAS)
Investments in Training Equipment
Funding for projects that demonstrate a need for trades training equipment and materials that meet industry standards or investments in new technology to train pre-apprentices and apprentices in Red Seal trades.
Up to $5,000,000 per project
- Accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- New Horizons for Seniors Program (NHSP) New Horizons for Seniors Program Community based projects (up to $50,000) Funds projects that empower seniors in their communities and contribute to improving their health and well-being. up to $50,000 Accepting applications until July 14, 2026 at 3:00 pm Eastern Standard Time (EST) Social Development and Community Building

#### New Horizons for Seniors Program (NHSP)
New Horizons for Seniors Program Community based projects (up to $50,000)
Funds projects that empower seniors in their communities and contribute to improving their health and well-being.
- Accepting applications until July 14, 2026 at 3:00 pm Eastern Standard Time (EST)
- Social Development and Community Building
- Canadian Benefit for Parents of Young Victims of Crime Parents of Young Victims of Crime Income support for parents or legal guardians who are considering to or have taken time away from work and suffered a loss of income to cope with the death or disappearance of your child or children. $500 per week Accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Canadian Benefit for Parents of Young Victims of Crime
Parents of Young Victims of Crime
Income support for parents or legal guardians who are considering to or have taken time away from work and suffered a loss of income to cope with the death or disappearance of your child or children.
- Accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Work-Sharing Worker Retention Grant for Work-Sharing Employers The Worker Retention Grant (WRG) is designed to enable employers with an approved and implemented Work-Sharing agreement to provide additional income support for their Employment Insurance (EI) eligible employees. This grant helps maintain employees’ income by keeping their earnings closer to their normal wages throughout the duration of the Work-Sharing agreement. More than $5,000,000 Accepting applications until December 31, 2026 at 3:00 pm Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) Jobs, training and education
Worker Retention Grant for Work-Sharing Employers
The Worker Retention Grant (WRG) is designed to enable employers with an approved and implemented Work-Sharing agreement to provide additional income support for their Employment Insurance (EI) eligible employees. This grant helps maintain employees’ income by keeping their earnings closer to their normal wages throughout the duration of the Work-Sharing agreement.
More than $5,000,000
- Accepting applications until December 31, 2026 at 3:00 pm Eastern Daylight Time (EDT)
- Jobs, training and education
- Youth Employment and Skills Strategy (YESS) Canada Summer Jobs Canada Summer Jobs (CSJ) provides wage subsidies to support employers to create quality summer work experiences for youth aged 15 to 30 years. Jobs, training and education

#### Youth Employment and Skills Strategy (YESS)
Canada Summer Jobs (CSJ) provides wage subsidies to support employers to create quality summer work experiences for youth aged 15 to 30 years.
- Jobs, training and education
- Youth Employment and Skills Strategy (YESS) Program Strategic Collaboration projects The Youth Employment and Skills Strategy (YESS) Program provides funding to organizations to deliver a range of activities that help youth (aged 15 to 30) overcome barriers to employment. Only specific organizations can apply. Not accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Youth Employment and Skills Strategy (YESS) Program
Strategic Collaboration projects
The Youth Employment and Skills Strategy (YESS) Program provides funding to organizations to deliver a range of activities that help youth (aged 15 to 30) overcome barriers to employment. Only specific organizations can apply.
- Not accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- Canada Service Corps (CSC) Accelerator Micro-grants Stream Funding for organizations and community groups that will design and deliver accelerator micro-grants to motivate altruistic behavior, help remove barriers, including financial ones, and to enable service objectives. Up to $5,000 Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Canada Service Corps (CSC)
Accelerator Micro-grants Stream
Funding for organizations and community groups that will design and deliver accelerator micro-grants to motivate altruistic behavior, help remove barriers, including financial ones, and to enable service objectives.
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Social Development Partnerships Program (SDPP-D) Accessible Canada – National AccessAbility Week Funding – 2025 and 2026 This funding opportunity aims to support projects that increase awareness through National Accessibility Week (NAAW) activities in 2025 and in 2026. It will fund projects that improve understanding and knowledge of Canadians on accessibility and disability inclusion, reduce stigma and attitudinal barriers towards persons with disabilities, and share best practices and lessons learned related to NAAW activities within the disability community. Up to $100,000 or $200,000 (with special requirements) for a project that is up to 2 years Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Social Development Partnerships Program (SDPP-D)
Accessible Canada – National AccessAbility Week Funding – 2025 and 2026
This funding opportunity aims to support projects that increase awareness through National Accessibility Week (NAAW) activities in 2025 and in 2026. It will fund projects that improve understanding and knowledge of Canadians on accessibility and disability inclusion, reduce stigma and attitudinal barriers towards persons with disabilities, and share best practices and lessons learned related to NAAW activities within the disability community.
Up to $100,000 or $200,000 (with special requirements) for a project that is up to 2 years
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Migrant Workers Support Program (MWSP) Airport orientation and community-based services The objective of this process is to fund projects that support migrant workers through orientation and referral services upon arrival at major Canadian airports. Up to $5 million Not accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Migrant Workers Support Program (MWSP)
Airport orientation and community-based services
The objective of this process is to fund projects that support migrant workers through orientation and referral services upon arrival at major Canadian airports.
- Not accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- Social Development Partnership Program (SDPP) Analysis and dissemination of the 2018 General social survey – Giving, volunteering and participating Funds 1 project to support not-for profit Organizations and community groups in analyzing and disseminating the data. This will help citizens, businesses, not-for profit Organizations and community groups and others to have a better evidence-based understanding of the nature of giving and volunteering in Canada. Up to $1,000,000 Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Social Development Partnership Program (SDPP)
Analysis and dissemination of the 2018 General social survey – Giving, volunteering and participating
Funds 1 project to support not-for profit Organizations and community groups in analyzing and disseminating the data. This will help citizens, businesses, not-for profit Organizations and community groups and others to have a better evidence-based understanding of the nature of giving and volunteering in Canada.
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy (CAS) Apprenticeship Completion Grant A grant for registered apprentices who successfully complete their apprenticeship training program, and get their journeyperson certification in a designated Red Seal trade. $2000 Not accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy (CAS)
Apprenticeship Completion Grant
A grant for registered apprentices who successfully complete their apprenticeship training program, and get their journeyperson certification in a designated Red Seal trade.
- Not accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy (CAS) Apprenticeship Incentive Grant A grant to help apprentices pay for expenses while training as an apprentice in a designated Red Seal trade. $1000 Not accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy (CAS)
Apprenticeship Incentive Grant
A grant to help apprentices pay for expenses while training as an apprentice in a designated Red Seal trade.
- Not accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy (CAS) Apprenticeship Service Funding to intermediaries that have the capacity to support SMEs to hire new, first-year apprentices. up to $72,500,000 per year Not accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Canadian Apprenticeship Strategy (CAS)
Apprenticeship Service
Funding to intermediaries that have the capacity to support SMEs to hire new, first-year apprentices.
up to $72,500,000 per year
- Not accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- Social Finance Fund (SFF) Become a social finance wholesaler Funding to accelerate the growth of Canada’s social finance market over 16 fiscal years (2021 to 2036). $50 million to $400 million over the first 5 fiscal years Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Social Finance Fund (SFF)
Become a social finance wholesaler
Funding to accelerate the growth of Canada’s social finance market over 16 fiscal years (2021 to 2036).
$50 million to $400 million over the first 5 fiscal years
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Canada Retraining and Opportunities Initiative (CROI) Canada Retraining and Opportunities Initiative - funding program The Canada Retraining and Opportunities Initiative supports community workforce planning and skills training in communities significantly impacted by a mass layoff.  It provides funding for community-based projects that help workers develop the skills they need to transition to new jobs. Not accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Canada Retraining and Opportunities Initiative (CROI)
Canada Retraining and Opportunities Initiative - funding program
The Canada Retraining and Opportunities Initiative supports community workforce planning and skills training in communities significantly impacted by a mass layoff.  It provides funding for community-based projects that help workers develop the skills they need to transition to new jobs.
- Not accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- Social Development Partnerhsip Program (SDPP) Community Services Recovery Fund National Funders to distribute the Community Services Recovery Fund to eligible charities and not-for-profit Community Service Organizations across Canada. This program will strengthen the sector as it supports Canadian communities to recover from the COVID-19 pandemic. Up to $397,540,000 Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Social Development Partnerhsip Program (SDPP)
Community Services Recovery Fund
National Funders to distribute the Community Services Recovery Fund to eligible charities and not-for-profit Community Service Organizations across Canada. This program will strengthen the sector as it supports Canadian communities to recover from the COVID-19 pandemic.
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- New Horizons for Seniors Program (NHSP) Community-based projects (up to $25,000) Funds projects that empower seniors in their communities and contribute to improving their health and well-being. Up to $25,000 Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### New Horizons for Seniors Program (NHSP)
Community-based projects (up to $25,000)
Funds projects that empower seniors in their communities and contribute to improving their health and well-being.
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- New Horizons for Seniors Program (NHSP) Community-based projects (up to $5,000) A federal grant that supports community-based projects that are designed by seniors for seniors and are volunteer based. This program funds projects that empower seniors in their communities and contribute to improving their health and well-being. Up to $5,000 Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### New Horizons for Seniors Program (NHSP)
Community-based projects (up to $5,000)
A federal grant that supports community-based projects that are designed by seniors for seniors and are volunteer based. This program funds projects that empower seniors in their communities and contribute to improving their health and well-being.
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Social Development Partnership Program (SDPP) Disability - Youth leadership Funds projects aimed at developing the next generation of youth disability leaders while also facilitating succession planning and sustainability in the disability sector.  Funding is for Organizations and community groups to form a partnership between a national disability organization and an organization within or outside of the disability sector, that is actively engaging youth. Up to $50,000 per year Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Social Development Partnership Program (SDPP)
Disability - Youth leadership
Funds projects aimed at developing the next generation of youth disability leaders while also facilitating succession planning and sustainability in the disability sector.  Funding is for Organizations and community groups to form a partnership between a national disability organization and an organization within or outside of the disability sector, that is actively engaging youth.
Up to $50,000 per year
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Social Development Partnership Program (SDPP) Disability Component - Disability Inclusion Action Plan Funding to improve the participation and integration of persons with disabilities in all aspects of Canadian society with respect to social inclusion. It will support disability stakeholder-led engagement activities on the Disability Inclusion Action Plan. In result of this process, one partnership-based agreement will be funded. Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Social Development Partnership Program (SDPP)
Disability Component - Disability Inclusion Action Plan
Funding to improve the participation and integration of persons with disabilities in all aspects of Canadian society with respect to social inclusion. It will support disability stakeholder-led engagement activities on the Disability Inclusion Action Plan. In result of this process, one partnership-based agreement will be funded.
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Social Development Partnership Program (SDPP) Early learning and child care ELCC funding supports early learning and child care through transfers to provinces and territories, projects that explore, test, and develop innovative approaches to support early learning and child care and through data and research. Up to  $1,000,000 Not accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Social Development Partnership Program (SDPP)
Early learning and child care
ELCC funding supports early learning and child care through transfers to provinces and territories, projects that explore, test, and develop innovative approaches to support early learning and child care and through data and research.
- Not accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- Early Learning and Child Care (ELCC) Early Learning and Child Care Innovation Program The ELCC Innovation Program provides funding to support organizations and projects aiming to explore, test and develop innovative approaches to child care as well as to improve the quality, accessibility, affordability, inclusivity and flexibility of early learning and child care programs and services. Up to $3,000,000 over the term of the project Not accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Early Learning and Child Care (ELCC)
Early Learning and Child Care Innovation Program
The ELCC Innovation Program provides funding to support organizations and projects aiming to explore, test and develop innovative approaches to child care as well as to improve the quality, accessibility, affordability, inclusivity and flexibility of early learning and child care programs and services.
Up to $3,000,000 over the term of the project
- Not accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- Enabling Accessibility Fund (EAF) Early Learning and Child Care small projects Funding to improve accessibility and safety in regulated and/or licensed ELCC centres. This will help support children with disabilities, parents/guardians with disabilities and/or employees with disabilities. The goal is to ensure that all Canadian families have access to high-quality, affordable and inclusive early learning and child care. Up to $25,000,000 in funding over 2 years Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Enabling Accessibility Fund (EAF)
Early Learning and Child Care small projects
Funding to improve accessibility and safety in regulated and/or licensed ELCC centres. This will help support children with disabilities, parents/guardians with disabilities and/or employees with disabilities. The goal is to ensure that all Canadian families have access to high-quality, affordable and inclusive early learning and child care.
Up to $25,000,000 in funding over 2 years
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Investment Readiness Program (IRP) Ecosystem builder (Round 2) Funds projects that help grow and strengthen the Social Innovation or Social Finance ecosystem. This will provide another layer of support to social purpose Organizations and community groups. Up to $45,815,426 Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Investment Readiness Program (IRP)
Ecosystem builder (Round 2)
Funds projects that help grow and strengthen the Social Innovation or Social Finance ecosystem. This will provide another layer of support to social purpose Organizations and community groups.
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Enabling Accessibility Fund (EAF) Enabling Accessibility Fund - Small Projects Component This funding opportunity aims to make communities and workplaces across Canada more accessible for persons with disabilities. It will fund projects that create more opportunities for persons with disabilities to take part in community activities, programs and services, and access employment. Up to $125,000 for a project that is up to 2 years in duration Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Enabling Accessibility Fund (EAF)
Enabling Accessibility Fund - Small Projects Component
This funding opportunity aims to make communities and workplaces across Canada more accessible for persons with disabilities. It will fund projects that create more opportunities for persons with disabilities to take part in community activities, programs and services, and access employment.
Up to $125,000 for a project that is up to 2 years in duration
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Enabling Accessibility Fund (EAF) Enabling Accessibility Fund — Youth Innovation Component - funding program The youth innovation component encourages youth to find accessibility barriers in their communities. They work with local organizations to improve accessibility and safety in community spaces and workplaces Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Enabling Accessibility Fund (EAF)
Enabling Accessibility Fund — Youth Innovation Component - funding program
The youth innovation component encourages youth to find accessibility barriers in their communities. They work with local organizations to improve accessibility and safety in community spaces and workplaces
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Enabling Accessibility Fund (EAF) Enabling Accessiblity Fund - Contribution This funding opportunity aims to make workplaces and communities across Canada more accessible for persons with disabilities. It will fund projects that create more opportunities for persons with disabilities to access and maintain employment. In addition, it will fund projects that remove accessibility barriers for persons with disabilities in Indigenous workplaces or Indigenous communities. $500,000 and a maximum of $1,000,000 for a project that is up to 2 years in duration Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Enabling Accessibility Fund (EAF)
Enabling Accessiblity Fund - Contribution
This funding opportunity aims to make workplaces and communities across Canada more accessible for persons with disabilities. It will fund projects that create more opportunities for persons with disabilities to access and maintain employment. In addition, it will fund projects that remove accessibility barriers for persons with disabilities in Indigenous workplaces or Indigenous communities.
$500,000 and a maximum of $1,000,000 for a project that is up to 2 years in duration
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Enabling Accessibility Fund (EAF) Enabling Fund for Official Language Minority Communities Funding to help official language minority communities grow and thrive. It supports them by helping national and regional Organizations and community groups develop the economy and human resources within these communities. It also encourages collaboration between community leaders and fosters partnerships to further these goals. Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

#### Enabling Accessibility Fund (EAF)
Enabling Fund for Official Language Minority Communities
Funding to help official language minority communities grow and thrive. It supports them by helping national and regional Organizations and community groups develop the economy and human resources within these communities. It also encourages collaboration between community leaders and fosters partnerships to further these goals.
- Not accepting applications
- Social Development and Community Building
- Employment Assistance Services Enabling Fund for Official Language Minority Communities - Employment Assistance Services This funding opportunity aims to support Official Language Minority Communities (OLMC) organizations to deliver Employment Assistance Services (EAS) to English-speaking communities in Quebec and Francophone communities outside Quebec in their first official language. EAS includes services such as providing job search assistance, counselling/case management, job placement services, and unassisted services to help individuals prepare for, obtain and/or maintain employment. Not accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Employment Assistance Services
Enabling Fund for Official Language Minority Communities - Employment Assistance Services
This funding opportunity aims to support Official Language Minority Communities (OLMC) organizations to deliver Employment Assistance Services (EAS) to English-speaking communities in Quebec and Francophone communities outside Quebec in their first official language. EAS includes services such as providing job search assistance, counselling/case management, job placement services, and unassisted services to help individuals prepare for, obtain and/or maintain employment.
- Not accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- Opportunities Fund for Persons with Disabilities (OF) Enhancing Access to Professional Sign Language Interpretation Funding projects that will increase access to American Sign Language (ASL) and to langue des signes québécoise (LSQ) interpretation. Up to $2,000,000 Not accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Opportunities Fund for Persons with Disabilities (OF)
Enhancing Access to Professional Sign Language Interpretation
Funding projects that will increase access to American Sign Language (ASL) and to langue des signes québécoise (LSQ) interpretation.
- Not accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- Opportunities Fund for Persons with Disabilities (OF) Enhancing Access to Professional Sign Language Interpretation - Step 2 – Solicited Proposals This funding initiative is through the Opportunities Fund for Persons with Disabilities Program. It aims to support projects that will enhance access to professional sign language interpretation. There is a lack of professional sign language interpreters in Canada. The Deaf community, or people whose main method of communication is sign language, don't always have access to sign language interpretation. The Accessible Canada Act has increased the demand for sign language interpretation.

The Act requires consultation with persons with disabilities, which includes the Deaf community, so more programs and services can be accessible. Up to $2,000,000 Not accepting applications Jobs, training and education

#### Opportunities Fund for Persons with Disabilities (OF)
Enhancing Access to Professional Sign Language Interpretation - Step 2 – Solicited Proposals
This funding initiative is through the Opportunities Fund for Persons with Disabilities Program. It aims to support projects that will enhance access to professional sign language interpretation. There is a lack of professional sign language interpreters in Canada. The Deaf community, or people whose main method of communication is sign language, don't always have access to sign language interpretation. The Accessible Canada Act has increased the demand for sign language interpretation.

The Act requires consultation with persons with disabilities, which includes the Deaf community, so more programs and services can be accessible.
- Not accepting applications
- Jobs, training and education
- Social Development Partnerships Program (SDPP-D) Equitable Access to Reading Program (EARP) – 2024 Call for Proposals This funding opportunity aims to increase the production of alternate format reading materials for persons with print disabilities. Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

Equitable Access to Reading Program (EARP) – 2024 Call for Proposals

Funding will be provided to Organizations and community groups to test new approaches, to assess what works and what does not and to share those findings with others who may be able to learn. Projects intended to improve the participation and integration of persons with disabilities in all aspects of their life. Up to $7,062,564 Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

Intersectional Capacity Development Stream

- Work Integration Social Enterprises (WISE) Longitudinal Research Projects (Phase II) Funding for research organizations to carry out a longitudinal study that answers specific policy research questions about social enterprises and the extent to which they contribute to the social and labour market integration of Black and racialized Canadians. These organizations would do so by delivering supports to youth and providing them with small, one-time cash payment. Up to $3,000,000  per year Not accepting applications Social Development and Community Building

- Social Development Partnership Program (SDPP) National operating funding stream - Disability Funding to support the capacity of national disability organizations to meaningfully and measurably increase the social inclusion of persons with disabilities in Canadian society. #### Social Development Partnership Program (SDPP)
